_comment: [44.0] MALTA -- the island as a PLACE with health, and the two-way loop that makes it one. Every magnitude here is transcribed from the 1979 book or read off the printed game-map; nothing is calibrated. This file exists because game/scenario.py used to carry a hand-typed calendar (_malta_bomb_points: 100/200/300/500/0/150/400 by month) that decided, more than any actual rule, who won the campaign -- invention I2 in scratchpad/port/00-THE-PORT-PLAN.md. Rule 44 replaces it: Maltese air-facility LEVELS -> 18 planes per level (44.14) -> the strike planes that reach the convoy lane -> the [41.5] CRT -> the percentage of the Axis convoy destroyed; and back the other way, the Axis spends from a FINITE Availability budget ([44.41], 64.52) to raid the island (44.21/44.24), knocking levels down and killing 10% of the planes on the ground per level (41.36), while the Commonwealth repairs on the [44.5] table (44.13).

air_facilities_44_12._comment: [44.12] 'Maltese air facilities are printed directly on the game-map (unlike most others). They are permanent and may never be moved. No further air facilities may be built on Malta. Although they may not be destroyed, Maltese air facilities may be reduced (by Axis bombing) to zero effectiveness.' PROVENANCE, AND IT IS NOT THE RULEBOOK: the Malta facilities are printed on GameMap 'A' (the off-scale Malta box, 44.11) and appear in NO chart in the book, so they were read off the game-map image shipped in CNAv2.1.0.vmod (Mitch Guthrie's 2021 redraw) -- the same source data/cna_map_grid.json and every terrain/road file already come from -- with hexes resolved through game.coords' Malta section 'M'. Each printed capacity matches the rulebook's own charted maximum for its kind (36.12 airfield six, 36.3 flying boat basin three, 36.4 alighting area one), and the total (28) contains every scenario's printed initial capacity (60.46 five, 61.34 eight, 62.36 fourteen, 63.37 'all facilities at full capacity'), which is the cross-check that the redraw is faithful here.

planes_per_level_44_14._rule: [44.14] 'The Commonwealth Player does not need -- nor does he use -- SGSU's on Malta. Each level of air facility can handle up to 18 planes of any type. Thus an airfield operating at maximum capacity (6 levels) could handle 108 planes.'

initial_setup_60_46._rule: [60.46] the campaign's own Malta set-up (64.3 sends the full campaign to Section 60 for every initial deployment). Verbatim: 'Planes: 15 Hurricane MkI's, 12 Swordfish, 3 Gladiators, 1 Sea Gladiator. AA: 17 AA Points. State of Air facilities: The Malta bases have an initial capacity of five SGSU's. These may be spread amongst the Malta facilities as the player wishes. Construction on increasing the capacity of Malta Air Facilities may begin in October, 1940.'
initial_setup_60_46._ready_comment: [60.46] prints a READY column beside each squadron and it is a live magnitude, not decoration: 44.16 'Planes based on Malta do not need fuel or ammo; they are automatically refueled and rearmed each Stage. HOWEVER, THEY MUST BE REFIT LIKE ALL OTHER PLANES, USING THE SAME METHOD AS ALL OTHER PLANES' -- i.e. the [38.37] Refit Table, which is what block 5.3 built. The printed rows, verbatim: '15 Hurricane MkI's (2 SGSU's) (12 Ready) | 12 Swordfish (1 SGSU) (9 ready) | 3 Gladiators (1 SGSU) (all ready) | 1 Sea Gladiator (1 SGSU) (all ready)'. So the campaign opens with three of Malta's twelve Swordfish unserviceable, and the island's strike force is a governed sortie rate rather than twelve aeroplanes over the lane every Game-Turn for 111 turns. The SGSU counts are transcribed because the book prints them, and nothing reads them: 44.14 says the Commonwealth 'does not need -- nor does he use -- SGSU's on Malta', and 60.46's parenthetical is 44.14's own 'for the sake of play ease... group his planes as if they were squadrons'.

strike_weapon._comment: [4.44A] Swordfish Mk. I, read with eyes off the scanned Commonwealth Bombers/Flying Boats/Transports/Reconnaissance chart (PDF p.113, re-rendered at 400 dpi for this repair): Range 48, TacAir (1), Maneuver 7, Bomb '-/T8', Fuel 1, mission capability B. (The row directly above it -- 'or 176 (5) 11 9 4' -- is the SUNDERLAND's second readying option, NOT a Swordfish bomber loadout; it was checked, because a Swordfish that could carry 9 Bomb Points would settle the ruling below.) The chart's own key: 'Bomb = Bombload Capacity and torpedo carrying capability, if any, in points. -- = May not carry bombs. Only planes possessing an /T may carry torpedos. The number to the left of the stroke is the Bombload Capacity. The number to the right of the T is the Torpedo Capacity.' B = 'Axis Naval Convoy and Land Support Bombing missions' -- the mission capability this use needs, printed on the row.
strike_weapon._index_scale_41_66: THE COLUMN IS THE BOMB POINTS COLUMN, AND THE CHART SAYS SO ITSELF. Footnote (a) of the [41.5] table -- the superscript printed on the 'Torpedo Points' header row on the foldout (PDF p.107) and spelled out in the Key on its sibling page (PDF p.108), rendered at 300 dpi and read with eyes -- is VERBATIM: 'a = Use only when attacking ships of the Commonwealth Fleet. Attacks consisting of Bomb and Torpedo Points are performed as two attacks. Attacks by planes armed with Torpedos against Ports or Axis Naval Convoys are carried out using the Bomb Points row (see Case 41.7).' Malta's Swordfish attacking an Axis Naval Convoy is exactly the case the footnote excludes from the Torpedo Points scale. The rule text agrees three ways: [41.72] scopes the torpedo line to 'Axis plane vs. Commonwealth Fleet'; [41.74] 'Torpedoes may be used against port facilities, but AS SUCH COUNT AS NORMAL BOMBS'; [41.66] the Commonwealth 'totals his attacking bomb points, MODIFYING FOR TORPEDOS IF NECESSARY (see Case 41.7)'. The modification is [41.73]: 'When Commonwealth planes fly against Axis convoys and at least 50% of the planes are carrying torpedoes, in determining the level (Case 41.66), INCREASE THE BOMBING TONNAGE BY 25%, ROUNDING UPWARD' -- printed a second time on the Key as 'Modications: If at least 50% of the planes attacking an Axis Naval Convoy are armed with Torpedos, increase the total Bomb Points by 25%.' Malta's strike is 100% torpedo-armed, so the modifier is always live.
strike_weapon._owner_ruling_settled: OWNER RULING MADE 2026-07-21 (Eve): THE TORPEDO READING IS CONFIRMED, NOT A GUESS -- the Swordfish's Torpedo Capacity of 8 IS its Bomb Points against an Axis Naval Convoy. THE QUESTION WAS: what number enters the Bomb Points row when the plane's Bombload Capacity is '-'? (a) [41.66] read strictly totals 'attacking bomb points', and [4.44A] gives the Swordfish Mk. I a Bombload of '-', so twelve of them would deliver 0 x 1.25 = 0, land below the table's [1..20] floor, and Malta's only 1940 anti-shipping aircraft could not scratch a convoy for the whole war -- against [41.71] ('torpedoes... are effective against ships') and against 44.0's entire premise. (b) [41.74] says a torpedo 'as such counts as normal bombs', which supplies the only conversion the book prints. THE OWNER VERIFIED BOTH HALVES ON THE SCAN: [4.44A] on PDF p.113 does read '-/T8' for the Swordfish, and the [41.5] Key on PDF p.108 prints, verbatim, 'Modiciations: If at least 50% of the planes attacking an Axis Naval Convoy are armed with Torpedos, INCREASE THE TOTAL BOMB POINTS BY 25%.' A twenty-five per cent increase of zero is meaningless, so the chart's own Key presupposes that a torpedo-armed plane contributes Bomb Points -- and 41.74 supplies the conversion. Reading (b) is therefore the book's, not a convenience. BEHAVIOUR DOES NOT CHANGE: the engine has applied (b) since rule 44 landed; what changes is that this is a RULED reading of the printed evidence rather than an open judgement call. Scan pages: PDF p.107 (the [41.5] foldout and its three index scales), PDF p.108 (the Key, footnote a and the +25% modification), PDF p.113 ([4.44A], the Swordfish row). Rule text: docs/rules/41-bombing-missions.md 41.66/41.71-41.75.

later_scenario_establishments._comment: NOTHING READS THIS. It is recorded as the evidence for the one gap this port leaves open in rule 44: Malta's plane establishment GROWS through the war (34.81A caps it -- 'no more than 10% of a month's airplane reinforcements may be sent to Malta' -- off the [34.86] Commonwealth Airplane Reinforcement and Squadron Withdrawal Schedule, which is untranscribed), and so does its facility capacity. Our campaign holds both at their printed September-1940 values, so our Malta only ever loses planes and levels. These four printed snapshots are what the growth actually looks like, and they are the acceptance test for whoever transcribes [34.86].

planes_on_the_ground_41_36._rule: [41.36] 'The result is the number of capacity levels that facility is reduced. In addition, FOR EVERY LEVEL DESTROYED, REMOVE 10% OF THE PLANES ON THE GROUND (e.g., 2 levels, 20% planes), rounded down.' The magnitude lives here rather than inline in game/malta.py for the same reason every other chart number in this port does: code reads magnitudes from data, never from literals.

construction_table_44_5._comment: [44.5] MALTESE AIR FACILITY CONSTRUCTION TABLE, transcribed and eyes-verified off the scan (PDF p.178, rendered at 400 dpi). 'Nr. Levels = Total number of levels of Air Facility repaired and/or constructed.' [44.13]: 'The initial capacity of Maltese air bases is given in each scenario. It may be increased -- up to the standard levels -- by using the Maltese Air Facility Construction Table (44.5). The Commonwealth Player may use the table ONCE EACH GAME-TURN FOR EACH AIR FACILITY on Malta... No supplies need be expended.'

strategic_commitment_44_41._comment: [44.41] AXIS STRATEGIC AIRFORCE COMMITMENT CHART, transcribed and eyes-verified off the scan (PDF p.178, rendered at 400 dpi). The chart's own key: '*Malta is not in the scenario. U = That availability level may be used an unlimited number of times. # = That availability level may only be used for that number of game turns of Axis Strategic Bombardment of Malta. na = Not applicable or not allowed.' [64.52]: 'The Axis Player's Malta Air Availability is determined using the appropriate campaign game row on the Axis Strategic Airforce Commitment Chart.' [44.23]: 'No Availability Level may ever be used more Game-Turns than it is listed as available. It is up to the Axis Player to plan his raids according to what he wants to accomplish and when he wants to do it.' null = unlimited (U); 0 = na.

availability_table_44_42._comment: [44.42] AXIS MALTA AVAILABILITY TABLE, transcribed and eyes-verified cell by cell off the scan (PDF p.178, rendered at 400 dpi over the table alone). Two dice ADDED (2..12; the chart's DICE column runs 2-12, unlike the [41.5] sequential-code tables). Each cell is [in_play_pct, strategic_pct]; null = the chart's 'na'. The chart's own key: 'na = No forces are available for the strategic bombardment of Malta this Game-Turn. Nr/ = That percentage of each type of planes IN PLAY AND BASED ON SICILY AND ITALY that may participate in strategic bombardment of Malta. /Nr = Used to determine the number of each type of plane available from the Axis Player's STRATEGIC FORCES (i.e., not actually in play) that may participate in that Game-Turn's raid on Malta missions. The planes available are calculated reading the number as a percentage of each type of the planes based on Italy/Sicily, rounding fractions down (e.g., 200 = 200%, or double the number of each type of plane in play).'

italy_sicily_basing_43_1._comment: [43.12] 'Until 1/35 Game-Turn 1941, 75% of all German bombers must be based in Italy/Sicily.' [43.13] 'From June 1/35 Game-Turn 1941 to the end of the game at least 50% of all He111's, Ju88's, and FW220's must be based in Crete; the remaining 25% may be based in Sicily/Italy or in Crete.' [43.25] 'Bombers based in Italy/Sicily may coordinate with African-based fighters for CAP. THEY MAY ALSO BE USED IN RAIDS ON MALTA (see 44.0)' -- and, by omission, Crete-based bombers may not. THIS BLOCK ARRIVED WITH RULE 44 (block 5.4) because [44.42]'s percentages are percentages OF THE PLANES BASED IN ITALY/SICILY, so the Axis half of rule 44 cannot be computed at all without a basing fraction; rule 43 proper has since landed (block 5.5) and game/basing.py is now the only reader, so the Malta raid's SIZING and the African battlefield's DEDUCTION are literally the same number. These are the book's own two numbers; the SECOND one is a permission ('may') rather than a requirement, so 25 is the printed CEILING on the post-June-1941 Italy/Sicily basing and our Axis takes it -- a policy choice, flagged, made where the alternative (0) would silently end the Malta war in June 1941.
italy_sicily_basing_43_1._comment_43_11: [43.11] 'At least 75% of all German He 111's, Ju88D's and FW220's must be based in MEDITERRANEAN BASES.' That is mediterranean_pct, and it is the total rule 43 takes OFF THE AFRICAN BATTLEFIELD. The engine does not read it: game/basing.py adds 43.12's Italy/Sicily share to 43.13's Crete share, which close on it exactly (75 + 0 before Game-Turn 35, 25 + 50 from it) under the reading where all three cases bind, and the number below is the transcription those two are checked against. WHAT THE ENGINE DEDUCTS FROM AFRICA IS EXACTLY WHAT IT BASES IN THE MEDITERRANEAN -- an aeroplane is in one place. RESTATED 2026-07-22, BECAUSE THE PROXY IS GONE AND THIS SENTENCE STILL DESCRIBED IT. The establishment these percentages are taken of is no longer 'one abstract Axis strike pool (a Ju. 87B)' -- air.REPRESENTATIVE_AIRCRAFT is deleted and the [34.6]/[59.3] Initial Air Strengths are transcribed (data/air_establishments.json, game/roster.py), so it is [60.32]'s 184 real bombers. And the subject of each percentage is now the one the rule names: 43.12 governs EVERY GERMAN BOMBER (game/basing.py:german_bombers, read off the chart's own nation cell) until Game-Turn 35, 43.11/43.13 govern the three NAMED heavies from it (game/basing.py:constrained_planes), and game/basing.py:required_planes is the switch between them. In the campaign every one of those populations is EMPTY -- [60.32] musters no German aeroplane -- so rule 43 takes nothing off the desert until the untranscribed [34.87] reinforcement schedule brings the Luftwaffe.
italy_sicily_basing_43_1._comment_constrained_types: [43.11]/[43.13] THE THREE TYPES RULE 43'S TYPED CASES NAME, GIVEN AS THE [4.44b] CHART PRINTS THEM. The rule's prose (PDF p.61) reads 'all German He 111's, Ju88D's and FW220's'; the [4.44b] German Aircraft Characteristics Chart (PDF p.145, rendered at 300 dpi and read with eyes) prints 'He. 111', 'Ju. 88D' and -- per the owner's ruling of 2026-07-21 that the rule's 'FW220' IS that aeroplane -- 'Fw. 200 C', periods and all; those printed names are the keys of aircraft_characteristics_4_44 in data/logistics_rates.json, which game/basing.py matches against EXACTLY. A list transcribed off the prose instead could never match a transcribed chart row and would have failed SILENTLY, which is what happened here once. RESTATED 2026-07-22: this key used to say the list was 'why 43.13's CRETE HALF DOES NOT CURRENTLY BITE: game.air expresses the whole abstract Axis strike pool as the Ju. 87B', and to send the reader to 'the OWNER RULING still open at game/basing.py:typed_requirement_applies'. BOTH CLAIMS ARE NOW FALSE. There is no representative-aircraft proxy; the establishment is [60.32]'s real muster; and that owner ruling DISSOLVED rather than being answered, because the muster contains no German aeroplane of ANY type, named or otherwise. 43.13's Crete half binds on nothing for a reason that is the book's (a missing reinforcement chart) and not ours (an abstraction). NOTE ALSO THAT THIS LIST IS NOT THE SUBJECT OF 43.12: that sentence says 'all German BOMBERS', untyped, and is applied to the nationality (game/basing.py:german_bombers), which is a strictly wider population -- [4.44b] also prints the Ju. 87B, the Ju. 87D and the Ju. 52/3m.
italy_sicily_basing_43_1._ruling_fw220_is_the_fw_200_c: OWNER RULING MADE 2026-07-21 (Eve): 'FW220' IS THE Fw. 200 C -- 'yes thats the same aircraft'. THE QUESTION, as it stood: 43.11 and 43.13 both name an aircraft they call 'FW220' (PDF p.61) and NO SUCH AIRCRAFT EXISTS IN THIS GAME -- the [4.44b] chart (PDF p.145) prints exactly eight German bombers/transports/recon types (Ar. 196, Fw. 200 C, He. 111, Hs. 126, Ju. 52/3m, Ju. 87B, Ju. 87D, Ju. 88D) and the nearest Focke Wulf bomber is the Fw. 200 C (Range 205, Bomb 14, Fuel 3). Whether the rule's 'FW220' was a misprint for it was a book-internal inconsistency of the same class as the 54.17 demolition table, so it was left UNSEEDED rather than guessed. The affirmative ruling is the whole of the change: 'Fw. 200 C' now stands in constrained_types_43_11 beside He. 111 and Ju. 88D, and 43.11/43.13 constrain all three. RESTATED 2026-07-22: when this ruling was recorded it added that the change 'moves no behaviour today -- the engine fields one abstract Axis bomber and it is a Ju. 87B, which is on none of the three rows'. That reason is obsolete (there is no representative-aircraft proxy any more) and the conclusion survives it for a better one: the [34.6]/[59.3] roster is now transcribed and [60.32] musters no German aeroplane of any type, so all three named rows wait together on the untranscribed [34.87] Axis Airplane Reinforcement Schedule. Note also that the Ju. 87B is NOT outside rule 43 on the strength of not being named -- it is a German bomber, so 43.12's untyped sentence governs it until Game-Turn 35 (game/basing.py:german_bombers).
italy_sicily_basing_43_1._owner_ruling_needed_60_32_vs_44_21: OWNER RULING NEEDED -- HOW MUCH OF HIS AIR FORCE DOES THE AXIS BASE IN ITALY/SICILY, WHEN THE SCENARIO PRINTS NONE AND RULE 44 REQUIRES SOME? This became a live question the moment the [34.6]/[59.3] Initial Air Strengths were transcribed (data/air_establishments.json), because the establishment they replaced was an abstract Axis bomber pool that rule 43's percentage was simply applied to whole. THE THREE PRINTED FACTS THAT COLLIDE: (a) [60.32], PDF p.78, read with eyes: 'The following planes may be placed at any Italian airfields, landing strips, flying boat basins, etc... However, NO PLANES START THE GAME IN ITALY/SICILY. Crete is unavailable (it is still British).' -- and [64.3] sends the campaign to section 60 for its air set-up, so that sentence is the campaign's. (b) [44.21] 'Axis bombers BASED IN ITALY/SICILY as well as any African-based Axis planes that can reach the island can raid Malta', [44.25]/[44.42] size the raid as a percentage of 'each type of planes IN PLAY AND BASED ON SICILY AND ITALY', and [44.27] caps the African contingent at what the table granted -- so with nothing in Sicily the table grants nothing, Africa may add nothing, and THE AXIS CAN NEVER RAID MALTA. (c) [64.52] and the campaign row of [44.41] nevertheless give him Availability Level I an UNLIMITED number of Game-Turns, Level II twenty-five, Levels III and IV twelve each; and [60.36] restricts the Italian campaign's raids to Level I, which presupposes he can fly them. THE BRIDGE THE BOOK INTENDS is 43.1's free basing choice -- [61.42] states it in the set-ups' own words, 'The Axis Player may base any portion of his airforce at Italy/Sicily within the minimum German plane restrictions of Case 43.1' -- together with a rule-37 transfer mission. THIS ENGINE HAS NEITHER: no transfer mission, no basing order, no air seat. THE POSTURE IS THEREFORE LEFT UNSEEDED (axis_discretionary_italy_sicily_pct_43_1 below is null, and game/basing.py:discretionary_pct answers 0). CORRECTED 2026-07-22, AND THE CORRECTION IS THE POINT OF THIS KEY. This block first shipped with 10 SEEDED here, transplanted from [63.46] -- the El Alamein scenario group's Axis Mediterranean Air Force Deployment, which prints 'The Axis Player may transfer 10% of his available force to Italy or Sicily but no more. HE MAY NOT HAVE MORE THAN 10% OF HIS AIR POWER IN ITALY OR SICILY.' Three things make that transplant the wrong shipping default rather than the cautious one: it is written for OCTOBER 1942 and applied to SEPTEMBER 1940; [63.46]'s VERY NEXT SENTENCE reads 'Strategic air attacks against Malta may be made only in the Long Retreat scenario (see 44.41)', so the 10% ceiling is printed for a posture in which Malta raiding is BARRED; and game/basing.py:italy_sicily_planes is the ONLY force rule 44 sizes a raid from, so that one integer decided the entire Malta half of the campaign -- a measurement of [63.46] reported as a measurement of [60.32]. The escalation protocol for a ruling only the owner can make is to implement what is safe and leave the disputed part clearly UNSEEDED, so it is unseeded, and the consequence is stated instead of hidden: WHILE THIS RULING IS OPEN THE CAMPAIGN AXIS RAIDS MALTA WITH NOTHING, which is attributable to the unbuilt rule-37 transfer channel and not to a repealed rule 44. THE THREE CANDIDATE ANSWERS, each one integer: 10 ([63.46]'s ceiling, transcribed unapplied below at axis_discretionary_italy_sicily_pct_63_46_unapplied); 0 (read (a) strictly -- the campaign's Axis never raids Malta, which is the current behaviour but as a RULING rather than as a gap); or 75/25 (apply 43.12's German-bomber requirement to Italian aeroplanes it does not govern, which was the shape of the old proxy and would strip three quarters of the Regia Aeronautica off the desert against [60.32]'s plain words). Scan pages: PDF p.78 ([60.32]/[60.36]), p.61 (43.1), p.61-62 (44.21-44.29), p.87 ([63.46] -- book folio 36, left column; docs/rules/63-scenario-group-four-el-alamein.md:369 sits between the page-087 and page-088 markers, and PDF p.88 is [63.92]/[64.0]), p.178 ([44.41]/[44.42]).
italy_sicily_basing_43_1._comment_discretionary_unapplied: [63.46] TRANSCRIBED AND UNAPPLIED, in the idiom this file already uses for the losing printing of [35.23] (data/logistics_rates.json play_aid_35_23_unapplied): NOTHING READS axis_discretionary_italy_sicily_pct_63_46_unapplied. It is the verbatim ceiling the owner ruling above weighs, kept beside the null so that answering the ruling is moving one integer up one line and not a re-reading of the scan.
